summary: Clear soundboard recording of a dust‑filled Oregon Country Fair show with scorching West LA Fadeaway, Day Job and Black Peter performances.
review: The August 28 1982 Oregon Country Fair set captures the Dead at a peak – Jerry’s guitar is on fire, Brent’s keys are hot, and the crowd rides extended jams on classics like West LA Fadeaway, Day Job and a searing Black Peter. The soundboard source is generally clear though a bit low‑level, with plenty of bottom end and no major dropouts.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 30 reviews overwhelmingly rate the show 4‑5 stars and highlight standout performances; recording is described as good/clear soundboard. Weighted average (70 % show, 30 % tape) yields a high rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally clear and balanced; some reviewers note the overall level is a bit low but the bottom end is strong. No reports of drop‑outs or major distortion.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The outdoor, dusty venue gave the show a relaxed, jam‑heavy vibe. Two stages and occasional dust storms caused an early cutoff on one account, but most listeners describe a flowing set with long stretches of improvisation and high energy throughout.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for his guitar tone, inventive turnarounds, envelope‑filter tricks and the way he “stretches out” on Black Peter and other jams.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass is described as so strong it “vibrated” the venue; solid foundation noted by several reviewers.
band_member_comments.Bob: Vocals are called intense, ‘fired up’, with many fans noting his “Bob songs” and powerful delivery on the second set.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland receives high marks for hot piano/organ work, especially on West LA and Althea; his playing is described as straight‑ahead and driving.
band_member_comments.Drums: Mickey Hart’s drumming is highlighted as “on fire” with notable Playin>Drums jams; the rhythm section is praised for tight, energetic support.
